name: Walnut Crescents #2
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Sweet butter, cut into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
Legend has it that bakers started making pastry in the form of crescents following the Turkish siege of Vienna in 1683.
Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit. In a large bowl, mix flour, sugar and ground walnuts. Make a well in the flour mixture and add the egg yolks. Gradually incorporate the butter pieces into the flour mixture until a dough forms. Knead thoroughly and shape into a ball. Add more flour as needed to prevent sticking. Divide the ball into 4 parts and roll each part into a roll about 1 inch thick. Cut 1/4-inch strips from the rolls, then form small crescents in the palm of your hand. Place the crescents on a greased baking sheet. Bake for 9-10 minutes or until lightly browned. Remove from oven while still warm and roll each crescent in confectioners sugar (handle gently as they break easily). Transfer to a wire rack to cool completely before storing in an airtight container. Yield: approximately 3 dozen.
